mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) create a 'stagflation' environment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to elevated risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) above 500k units signal robust consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds 500k, or the signal's IC falls below 0.04 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 95.0 signals improving manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 95.0, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sustained corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS and GDPC1 both exceed thresholds, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k signals weak labor market performance, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS falls below 100k, or the signal's IC drops below -0.04 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.0% signals central bank policy inaction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through rising real interest rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's IC falls below -0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.
